name: Train DeepLabCut Model

params.name: Labeled Data Zip File
params.description: A zip file containing the labeled data to use for training.

params.name: Config File
params.description: A config file describing the configuration of the DLC project. Includes the body parts and skeleton of the animal pose, training fraction, network and augmenter types, etc.

params.name: Engine
params.description: Specifies the backend engine to use for training the network. Either "tensorflow" or "pytorch". If empty, reads the engine value in the input config.yaml. DeepLabCut recommends using pytorch since it is faster. The tensorflow backend will be deprecated by the end of 2024.

params.name: Training Fraction
params.description: The fraction of labeled data to use for training the network, the remainder is used for testing the network for validation. This value is automatically calculated if “Train Video Files” and “Test Video Files” are specified.  If empty, and no “Train Video Files” and “Test Video Files” are specified, then the value in the input config file is used.

params.name: P Cutoff
params.description: Cutoff threshold for the confidence of model results. If used, then results with a confidence below the cutoff are omitted from analysis. This is used when generating evaluation results, and can be used downstream to filter analyses on novel videos.

params.name: Train Files
params.description: Comma-separated list of video file names from labeled data to use for training the network. Must be specified if “Test Video Files” is passed. If empty, then train images are automatically selected from labeled data.

params.name: Test Files
params.description: Comma-separated list of video file names from labeled data to use for testing the network. Must be specified if “Train Video Files” is passed. If empty, then test images are automatically selected from labeled data.

params.name: Net Type
params.description: Type of network. Currently supported options are: resnet_50, resnet_101, resnet_152, mobilenet_v2_1.0, mobilenet_v2_0.75, mobilenet_v2_0.5, mobilenet_v2_0.35, efficientnet-b0, efficientnet-b, efficientnet-b2, efficientnet-b3, efficientnet-b4, efficientnet-b5, efficientnet-b6

params.name: Augmenter Type
params.description: Type of augmenter. Currently supported options are: default, scalecrop, imgaug, tensorpack, deterministic

params.name: Train Pose Config File
params.description: A config file describing the configuration settings for training the network.

params.name: Pytorch Config File
params.description: A config file describing the pytorch configuration settings for training the network.

params.name: Number of Snapshots To Evaluate
params.description: Sets how many snapshots are evaluated, i.e. states of the trained network. Every saving iteration a snapshot is stored, however only the last “Number of Snapshots to Evaluate” are kept. If "all", then all snapshots are evaluated.

params.name: Save Iterations
params.description: The interval to save an iteration as a snapshot. If empty, then the value in pose_config.yaml is used.

params.name: Max Iterations
params.description: The maximum number of iterations to train the model. If empty, then the value in pose_config.yaml is used.

params.name: Allow Growth
params.description: For some smaller GPUs, memory issues happen. If true then, the memory allocator does not pre-allocate the entire specified GPU memory region, instead starting small and growing as needed. See issue: https://forum.image.sc/t/how-to-stop-running-out-of-vram/30551/2

params.name: Keep Deconvolution Weights
params.description: Restores the weights of the deconvolution layers (and the backbone) when training from a snapshot. Note that if you change the number of bodyparts, you need to set this to false for re-training.

params.name: Device
params.description: (pytorch only) The torch device to train on (such as "cpu", "cuda", "mps", "auto"). Overrides the value set in pytorch_config.yaml.

params.name: Batch Size
params.description: (pytorch only) The batch size to train with. Overrides the value set in pytorch_config.yaml. Small batch sizes (i.e., value of one) is good for training on a CPU, but this tool runs on a GPU instance. For GPUs a larger batch size should be used. The value should be the biggest power of 2 where you don't geta CUDA out-of-memory error, such as 8, 16, 32 or 64. This also allows you to increase the learning rate (empirically you can scale the learning rate by sqrt(batch_size) times). By default a batch size of 2 is used as this has shown to stay within memory limits on the instance used for this tool.

params.name: Epochs
params.description: (pytorch only) The maximum number of epochs to train the model. Overrides the value set in pytorch_config.yaml.

params.name: Save Epochs
params.description: (pytorch only) The number of epochs between each snapshot save. Overrides the value set in pytorch_config.yaml.

params.name: Detector Batch Size
params.description: (pytorch only) Only for top-down models. The batch size with which to train the detector. Overrides the value set in pytorch_config.yaml.

params.name: Detector Epochs
params.description: (pytorch only) Only for top-down models. The maximum number of epochs to train the model. Overrides the value set in pytorch_config.yaml. Setting to 0 means the detector will not be trained.

params.name: Detector Save Epochs
params.description: (pytorch only) Only for top-down models. The number of epochs between each snapshot of the detector is saved. Overrides the value set in pytorch_config.yaml.

params.name: Pose Threshold
params.description: (pytorch only) Used for memory-replay. pseudo predictions that are below this are discarded for memory-replay.

params.name: Test Pose Config File
params.description: A config file describing the configuration settings for testing (evaluating) the network.

params.name: Plot Evaluation Results
params.description: Plots the predictions of evaluation results on the train and test images.

params.name: Body Parts To Evaluate
params.description: The average error for evaluation results will be computed for those body parts only. The provided list has to be a subset of the defined body parts. Otherwise, set to “all” to compute error over all body parts.

params.name: Rescale
params.description: Evaluate the model at the 'global_scale' variable (as set in the pose_config.yaml file for a particular project). I.e. every image will be resized according to that scale and prediction will be compared to the resized ground truth. The error will be reported in pixels at rescaled to the original size. I.e. For a [200,200] pixel image evaluated at global_scale=.5, the predictions are calculated on [100,100] pixel images, compared to 1/2*ground truth and this error is then multiplied by 2. The evaluation images are also shown for the original size.

params.name: Per Keypoint Evaluation
params.description: Compute the train and test RMSE for each keypoint, and save the results to a {model_name}-keypoint-results.csv in the evalution-results folder

params.name: Generate Maps
params.description: Plot the scoremaps, locref layers, and PAFs from the evaluation results.

params.name: Optimal Snapshot Metric
params.description: The type of error from the evaluation results to minimize in order to find the optimal snapshot. Options are: Train Error, Test Error, Train Error w/ p-cutoff, Test Error w/ p-cutoff

params.name: Save Optiomal Snapshot
params.description: Only keep the snapshot with the smallest error from the evaluation results in the zipped model output. The error to minimize is indicated by the “Optimal Snapshot Error Metric” parameter.  This significantly reduces the size of the zipped model output uploaded to IDEAS.  If set to false, then all snapshots evaluated are included in the zipped model output. This is controlled by the “Snapshots to Evaluate” parameter.

results.name: Model
results.description: A zip file of the dlc-models folder from the DLC project which contains the model trained by DLC. The zip file will also contain some summary evaluation_results from the DLC project.

results.name: Evaluation Results
results.description: A zip file containing evaluation results of the trained model including plots of model predictions on labeled data, scoremaps, and locref maps.

description: Train a deep learning model for pose estimation using DeepLabCut
